objective: Analyze professional Brazilian restaurant website design patterns vs AI-generated look

sites_analyzed.segment: American/burger restaurant - 40+ years, 14 locations in São Paulo

color_palette.accent: Warm tones from food photography (reds, oranges, browns)
color_palette.background: White (#ffffff) for content, dark overlays on images
color_palette.text: Dark text on light backgrounds, white text on dark/image backgrounds
color_palette.observation: Colors are driven by photography rather than a strict brand palette - the food IS the color scheme

typography.observation: Mix of slab serif for headings (warmth, tradition) with clean sans-serif for readability. NOT using generic fonts like Arial

navigation_style.behavior: Anchor links to sections on single page

image_usage.hero: Large, professional food photography with shallow depth of field
image_usage.quality: HIGH - professional food photography, proper lighting, appetizing styling

what_makes_it_look_professional: Professional food photography with consistent style (warm tones, shallow DOF)
Real brand with 40 years of history - specific, authentic content
Multiple real locations with specific addresses and hours
Integration with iFood (real delivery platform)
Social media links to active accounts (@americaburger)
Real email addresses for different departments
WhatsApp plugin for instant contact
Specific promotional content (Colombos promotion with PDF regulation)
Reservation system link (usetag.me)
Cookie consent banner (GDPR compliance)
Google Analytics and Ads tracking
Subtle animations (hover effects, slider transitions)
Responsive design with mobile menu
what_would_make_it_look_AI_generated: Generic stock food photos instead of actual restaurant food
Perfectly balanced color palette (real sites are messier)
No real social proof (reviews, press mentions)

sites_analyzed.fetch_status: partial - blocked by server, data from search results and cached pages
sites_analyzed.segment: Brazilian sertaneja food - since 1973, Michelin Bib Gourmand, 63rd best in Latin America

color_palette.primary: Earth tones - warm browns, terracotta, cream
color_palette.text: Dark brown/charcoal on light, cream on dark
color_palette.observation: Earth tones that evoke the Sertão (Brazilian countryside) - colors match the brand story perfectly

typography.special_effect: Mixed capitalization pattern: 'o r e sTauRante', 'meNu', 't eaM'
typography.observation: HIGHLY DISTINCTIVE typography - the mixed case is intentional brand personality. This is NOT something AI would generate. It's a design choice that makes the brand memorable

navigation_style.observation: Site prioritizes content over navigation - editorial approach

image_usage.team: Real chef photos with authentic backstories
image_usage.observation: Images tell a story of authenticity and craft

what_makes_it_look_professional: Authentic brand story spanning 50+ years (since 1973)
Real awards and recognition (Michelin Bib Gourmand, World Restaurant Awards, 63rd in Latin America)
Specific, unique typography choices (mixed case) that show deliberate brand personality
Real team members with genuine backstories
Pet-friendly policy with specific rules (shows real operational detail)
Gift card system with real terms and conditions
Job application form with specific questions
Designed by a real agency (galpaodecriacao.com.br)
Multiple locations with specific addresses
Reservation system integration
Dual language support (Portuguese/English)
Editorial content approach - not just selling, telling a story
what_would_make_it_look_AI_generated: Perfectly consistent typography (the mixed case proves it's human-designed)
Stock photos instead of real team photos
Generic 'about us' text without specific awards/details

sites_analyzed.segment: Traditional Italian restaurant in São Paulo
sites_analyzed.notes: Could not directly access the website. Analysis based on domain patterns and context from similar traditional Italian restaurants in SP

professional_patterns: Colors emerge FROM the brand story (Mocotó = Sertão earth tones)
Not perfectly balanced - intentional asymmetry
Photography drives the color scheme (food IS the palette)
Muted/warm tones for restaurants (not neon/bright)
2-3 core colors max, rest comes from photography
ai_generated_tells: Too many colors or too-perfect gradients
Neon or overly saturated primary colors
Colors that don't relate to the food/cuisine type
Every section has a different background color

professional_patterns: 1-2 font families maximum
Serif for headings (warmth, tradition) + Sans-serif for body (readability)
Intentional, quirky typography choices (Mocotó's mixed case)
Font weights used strategically (bold for emphasis, light for secondary)
Letter-spacing adjustments for uppercase text
Google Fonts loaded efficiently
ai_generated_tells: Perfectly matching fonts that feel generic
Font choices that don't match the brand personality

professional_patterns: Single-page with anchor navigation (Restaurante America)
Editorial/storytelling layout (Mocotó)
Hero = food photography, NOT stock images
Content flows logically: Story → Food → Locations → Contact
Whitespace used generously
Boxed content within full-width sections
Sticky header that transforms on scroll
Mobile-first responsive design
ai_generated_tells: Every section has identical card layouts
Symmetrical grids with no visual hierarchy
Stock photography instead of real photos

professional_patterns: Specific, factual claims (40 years, 14 locations, 63rd best)
Real awards and certifications (Michelin, World Restaurant Awards)
Authentic brand voice with personality
Real addresses, phone numbers, emails
Integration with real platforms (iFood, GetInApp, Cielo)
Legal documents (promotion regulations, terms and conditions)
Department-specific contact info
Real team member profiles with genuine stories
ai_generated_tells: Generic marketing speak ('best food in town')
Perfectly polished copy with no personality

professional_signals: Social media links to active accounts

must_have_elements: Authentic brand story with specific details
Actual location(s) with address and hours
Social media links to real accounts
design_principles: Let photography drive the color palette
Tell a story, don't just sell
common_sections_for_restaurants: Menu / Cardápio (with prices or link to PDF)
